back_translation: When Jesus went out from the temple and was going [on his way], his disciples came to him to point out to him (for him to gaze upon) the buildings of the temple.
He answered them, "Do you see all these things? I tell you the truth: there will not be any stone left resting on another here; every stone will be torn down."

While he was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and asked, "Please tell us: when will these things occur, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?"
Jesus answered them, "Take care that no one leads you astray.
For many will come claiming my name, saying, 'I am the Christ,' and will lead many astray.
You will hear news of wars and rumors of war. Take care — do not be alarmed or troubled. For these things must happen, but the end has not yet come.
For nation will rise up against nation, and kingdom will rise up against kingdom; there will be famines and earthquakes in various places.
But all these things are the beginning of the pains of childbirth.
At that time they will hand you over to suffer tribulation and will kill you, and you will be hated by every nation because of my name.
At that time many will stumble and fall, and will betray and hate one another.
And many false prophets will appear, and will lead many astray.
And because lawlessness will multiply, the love of many will grow cold.
But the one who endures to the end, that one will be saved.
And this gospel concerning the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the whole world as a testimony to every nation, and then the end will come.
Therefore, when you see 'the abomination that causes desolation' of which Daniel the prophet spoke, standing in the holy place (let the reader understand),
at that time, those who are in the region of Judea must flee to the mountains.
The one who is on the rooftop must not come down to fetch things from his house.
And the one who is in the field must not return to fetch his outer garment.
In those days, it will be very sorrowful for pregnant women and for mothers nursing their babies.
Pray that your flight will not happen in winter or on a Sabbath day.
For at that time there will be great tribulation, of a kind that has never been from the beginning of the creation of the world until now, and will never be again.
And if those days had not been shortened [by Him], no human being would be saved. But for the sake of those whom He has chosen, those days will be shortened [by Him].
At that time, if anyone says to you, 'Look, the Christ is here,' or 'He is there,' do not believe it.
For false Christs and false prophets will appear, and they will display great signs and wonders in order to lead astray even those whom He has chosen, if it is possible.
Behold, I have told you beforehand.
Therefore, if they say to you, 'Look, he is in the wilderness,' do not go out; 'Look, he is in the inner rooms,' do not believe it.
For just as lightning shines from the east to the west — so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
Wherever there is a corpse, there the vultures will gather.
Immediately after the tribulation of those days, 'the sun will grow dark, and the moon will not shine its light; the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.'

At that time, the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky, and every tribe of the earth will beat their breasts and mourn. They will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with great power and glory.
He will send forth his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather those whom He has chosen from the four winds, from one end of the heavens to the other end.
Learn a parable from the fig tree: when its branch becomes tender and puts out leaves, you know that summer is near.
Likewise, when you see all these things, know that it is near, right at the door.
I tell you the truth: the people of this generation will not pass away until all these things happen.
The heavens and the earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
But about that day and hour, no one knows — not even the angels in the heavens know, not even the Son knows, but only the Father knows.
For just as in the days of Noah, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
For in the days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving their daughters in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the great boat.
And they were not aware at all, until the flood came and swept them all away. So also will be the coming of the Son of Man.
At that time two people will be in the field: one will be taken, and the other will be left.
Two women will be grinding flour at the millstone: one will be taken, and the other will be left.
Therefore, keep watch, because you do not know on what day your Lord will come.
But know this: if the owner of the house had known in which watch of the night the thief was coming, he would have kept watch, and would not have allowed his house to be broken into.
For this reason, you yourselves must also be ready, because at an hour you do not expect, the Son of Man will come.
Then who is the servant who is faithful and wise, whom the master has appointed to oversee the household-slaves in his house, to distribute food to them at the proper time?
Blessed is that servant, when his master returns and finds him doing so.
I tell you the truth: the master will appoint him to oversee all his possessions.
But if that wicked servant says in his heart, 'Our master is delaying and has not yet returned,'
and begins to beat his fellow servants, and eats and drinks with drunkards,
the master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect, and at an hour he does not know,
and [the master] will cut him in two and assign his place together with the hypocrites; there, there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
